raw_output:
## RDKit molecular descriptors (MW, LogP, TPSA, HBD, HBA)

(Computed with RDKit: `Descriptors.MolWt`, `Crippen.MolLogP`, `rdMolDescriptors.CalcTPSA`, `CalcNumHBD`, `CalcNumHBA`.)

| Molecule | SMILES | MW | LogP | TPSA | HBD | HBA |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Acetaminophen | CC(=O)Nc1ccc(O)cc1 | 151.165 | 1.351 | 49.33 | 2 | 2 |
| Metformin | CN(C)C(=N)NC(=N)N | 129.167 | -1.034 | 88.99 | 4 | 2 |
| Warfarin | CC(=O)CC(c1ccccc1)c2c(O)c3ccccc3c(=O)o2 | 308.333 | 3.610 | 67.51 | 1 | 4 |

## PubMed: RDKit-based descriptor analysis for ADMET prediction
The PubMed subtask results were not included in the worker outputs you provided (subtask_3 content is missing). If you paste the worker’s PubMed list (or allow me to perform the PubMed search directly), I will format 5–10 relevant papers with title, year, first author, journal, and PMID.

## Required computed summary (Lipinski RO5, mean LogP, highest TPSA)

ACETAMINOPHEN_VIOLATIONS: 0, METFORMIN_VIOLATIONS: 0, WARFARIN_VIOLATIONS: 0, MEAN_LOGP: 0.77, HIGHEST_TPSA: METFORMIN

subtasks.description: Using RDKit in python-exec, compute five descriptors (MW, LogP, TPSA, HBD, HBA) for Acetaminophen (CC(=O)Nc1ccc(O)cc1), Metformin (CN(C)C(=N)NC(=N)N), and Warfarin (CC(=O)CC(c1ccccc1)c2c(O)c3ccccc3c(=O)o2). Return a table/structured output with descriptor values per molecule.

Using the RDKit-computed descriptors, calculate in python-exec: (1) Lipinski RO5 violation count for each molecule (violation if MW>500, LogP>5, HBD>5, HBA>10), (2) mean LogP across the three compounds (rounded to 2 decimals), and (3) identify which molecule has the highest TPSA. Provide the exact final strings needed: ACETAMINOPHEN_VIOLATIONS: N, METFORMIN_VIOLATIONS: N, WARFARIN_VIOLATIONS: N, MEAN_LOGP: X.XX, HIGHEST_TPSA: <NAME>.

Search PubMed for papers on RDKit-based molecular descriptor analysis used for ADMET prediction. Compile a short list of relevant publications (e.g., 5–10) including title, year, first author, journal, and PubMed ID, emphasizing explicit use of RDKit descriptors/features in ADMET/toxicity/PK prediction workflows.

Return a table/structured output with descriptor values per molecule.
